explicit_text: My family originally comes from the Dominican Republic. My dad worked there as a master electrician, teaching trades to prisoners before they were released. He married my mom there and then brought her back with him. My grandfather also served in the Dominican Army, so the country has a strong connection to my family history.

explicit_text: I joined the United States Navy right after high school in July 1987. I went through boot camp in Orlando, Florida, and then specialized training in Pensacola, Millington, Memphis, and Jacksonville. I served as an acoustic operator on the P-3 Orion, flying missions and handling classified operations. The Navy was a big part of my life during the late 1980s and early 1990s, and it shaped my career and skills.

explicit_text: I served as an acoustic operator on the P-3 Orion, a plane with phenomenal range that allowed us to fly missions lasting up to 12 or 13 hours. We used the P-3 for reconnaissance, surveillance, and tracking submarines, especially Soviet ones during the Cold War. Operating the P-3 was a challenging and technical role that involved both flying and handling classified equipment.

Much of our work involved tracking Soviet submarines and conducting surveillance to ensure they stayed within limits.
